reactant_pattern_text: acyclic or cyclic alpha-diketone under basic conditions
product_pattern_text: alpha-hydroxy carboxylic acid salt or ring-contracted alpha-hydroxy acid
key_reagents_clue: NaOH, alkoxides, amide bases, hydroxide attack on diketones
synonym_names: benzilic acid rearrangement; retro-benzilic acid rearrangement
description_short: Base-induced rearrangement of alpha-diketones to alpha-hydroxy acid derivatives with migratory ring contraction variants.

reactant_pattern_text: aldehydes, glyoxals, or alpha-hydroxy ketones under cyanide or thiazolium catalysis
product_pattern_text: benzoin products or retro-benzoin cleavage products such as ketones and aldehydes
key_reagents_clue: KCN, NaCN, thiazolium salts, NHC catalysts, DBU
common_conditions: cyanide catalysis or thiazolium catalysis with proton-transfer sequence
description_short: Classical umpolung coupling of aldehydes to benzoins and the reverse fragmentation logic used for ketone synthesis.

product_pattern_text: benzenoid diradical trapped to an aromatic product
key_reagents_clue: heat, cyclohexadiene hydrogen donor, MgCl2, benzene
common_conditions: thermal or metal-induced cycloaromatization of enediynes
description_short: Cycloaromatization of enediynes to benzenoid diradicals that rapidly abstract hydrogen atoms and build aromatic frameworks.

key_reagents_clue: HCl, FeCl3, BF3·OEt2, Yb(OTf)3, urea, thiourea
common_conditions: acid- or Lewis-acid-catalyzed one-pot three-component condensation
description_short: Three-component condensation used to build dihydropyrimidinones from aldehydes, beta-keto esters, and urea derivatives.

reactant_pattern_text: arene or electron-poor heteroarene with alkali metal in liquid ammonia and alcohol
key_reagents_clue: Li or Na, NH3(l), ROH, t-BuOH, EtOH
common_solvents: liquid ammonia, THF co-solvent, alcohol proton sources
common_conditions: low-temperature dissolving-metal reduction; optional Birch reductive alkylation
description_short: Dissolving-metal reduction of aromatic rings and heteroarenes to unconjugated dienes or partially reduced heterocycles.
